registered: 2026-07-10, Phase 0 — BEFORE running the real model. Predictions are the occupancy NULL (uniform routing); the measurement tests deviation from it.
read_fraction_def: mean over layers of (distinct experts gathered in one forward / n_experts_total)

null_model.name: occupancy / coupon-collector, UNIFORM routing, tokens independent

headline_finding_from_analysis: Under the null, read_fraction is 1.0000 at every cell of the handoff's DEFAULT grid (batch 1-64 x seq 1024-4096, all >= 1024 eff_tokens). The crossover, if it exists, lives at eff_tokens in [1, ~128] — decode / sub-sequence scale, NOT the batch-scaling grid. The grid is reframed to a token-count sweep accordingly (see predictions[]).
hypothesis_under_test: Real routing is NOT uniform: adjacent tokens in a sequence route to correlated experts, and learned load-imbalance concentrates mass. Both make the real union grow SLOWER than the null -> real read_fraction sits BELOW 1 - 0.9375^n, and the 0.9 crossing moves to HIGHER eff_tokens than 35.7. The measurement quantifies that gap. If instead real ~= null, the sparse-read window is decode-scale and the SSD-tier thesis's useful regime is small-batch serving (a sharp, honest scoping result -> D2 becomes load-bearing).
grid: eff_tokens swept via batch=1 x seq in {1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128,256,512,1024,2048}; plus batch>1 checks at eff_tokens 64 and 512 (batch=8 x seq 8 and 64) to test whether cross-sequence batching gathers a WIDER union than one long sequence of equal token count — i.e. does within-sequence correlation matter vs across-batch independence.

predictions.note: same eff_tokens as batch1xseq64 — batch>1 vs long-seq comparison

deliverable: access_pattern.jsonl -> read_fraction vs eff_tokens (measured), overlaid on the null; the eff_tokens where MEASURED read_fraction crosses 0.5 and 0.9 are the only vertical slices Session 4 pays to measure.
second_curve_rationale_D2: OLMoE (E=64) crosses 0.9 at ~17 tokens (even narrower); a hypothetical 256-expert stack at ~73, 512-expert at ~146. The wide-window regime the SSD-tier thesis leans on requires MANY experts; 128 is already narrow. If 30B measures ~null, only a >=256-expert stack demonstrates a usefully wide window — which is the quantitative argument for D2.
